enonce.texte: Match each word to its correct definition. Draw a line from the word to the definition.

questions.texte: Match the vocabulary words to their definitions.
questions.reponse: 1. outbreak - c. A sudden start of a disease; 2. transmission - e. The spread of a disease from one person to another; 3. symptoms - d. Signs of an illness; 4. prevention - a. Actions taken to stop something from happening; 5. hygiene - b. Practices to keep clean and healthy

sousQuestions.texte: What is the difference between an outbreak and transmission?
sousQuestions.reponse: An outbreak is a sudden increase in cases of a disease. Transmission is how the disease spreads.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct word from the box: outbreak, transmission, symptoms, prevention, hygiene.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct word.

sousQuestions.texte: There was a serious disease ______ in the town.
The first ______ of the disease appeared quickly.
The ______ of the disease happened through contaminated water.
Good ______ is the best way to avoid getting sick.
Washing your hands regularly is a good ______ practice.

enonce.texte: Read the sentences. Identify which are Type 1 conditional sentences.

questions.texte: Choose the sentences that are Type 1 conditionals.

sousQuestions.texte: If you wash your hands regularly, you reduce the risk of infection.
If the disease spreads, the government will close the schools.
If I had known, I would have helped.
If you take the medicine, you will feel better.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences as Type 1 conditional sentences.

questions.texte: Rewrite the sentences using Type 1 conditionals.
questions.reponse: 1. If you eat healthy food, you will stay strong; 2. If you don't get enough rest, you will feel tired; 3. If the outbreak continues, many people will get sick

sousQuestions.texte: Eat healthy food. Stay strong.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you eat healthy food, you will stay strong.

sousQuestions.texte: Don't get enough rest. Feel tired.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you don't get enough rest, you will feel tired.

sousQuestions.texte: The outbreak continues. Many people get sick.
sousQuestions.reponse: If the outbreak continues, many people will get sick.

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions.

Responding to a Disease Outbreak

A disease outbreak can happen suddenly and cause serious health problems. For example, a recent outbreak of cholera in a rural community was caused by contaminated water. The first symptoms of cholera are watery diarrhea and vomiting. Without treatment, the disease can lead to severe dehydration and death. The government responded by providing clean water and medicine to the affected area. Health workers also educated people about the importance of good hygiene. If you wash your hands regularly, you reduce the risk of infection. If the disease spreads further, more people will get sick. Therefore, it is important to act quickly and effectively.

questions.texte: Answer the questions based on the text.
questions.reponse: 1. Cholera; 2. Contaminated water; 3. Watery diarrhea and vomiting; 4. Providing clean water and medicine, and educating people; 5. Washing hands regularly

sousQuestions.texte: What disease outbreak is mentioned in the text?
What caused the outbreak?
What are the first symptoms of cholera?
How did the government respond?
sousQuestions.reponse: By providing clean water and medicine, and educating people about hygiene.

sousQuestions.texte: What is mentioned as a way to reduce the risk of infection?

enonce.texte: You are a health worker in a community where a disease outbreak has just been reported. Write a short text (5-7 sentences) to educate the community about prevention measures. Use at least one Type 1 conditional sentence.

questions.reponse: Example: Dear community members, we have received reports of a cholera outbreak. To protect yourselves and your families, follow these important measures. Always drink clean, boiled water. Wash your hands with soap and water before eating. If you do not have clean water, you can use chlorine tablets. If you experience symptoms like diarrhea, go to the health center immediately. Remember, if you take these precautions, you will reduce the risk of getting sick. Stay safe!

sousQuestions.texte: What is the first prevention measure you mention?
What should people do if they have symptoms?
sousQuestions.reponse: Go to the health center immediately.

sousQuestions.texte: Write a Type 1 conditional sentence from your text.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you take these precautions, you will reduce the risk of getting sick.

enonce.texte:
Read the text again. Identify all the Type 1 conditional sentences and explain what real possibility they express.

Responding to a Disease Outbreak

A disease outbreak can happen suddenly and cause serious health problems. For example, a recent outbreak of cholera in a rural community was caused by contaminated water. The first symptoms of cholera are watery diarrhea and vomiting. Without treatment, the disease can lead to severe dehydration and death. The government responded by providing clean water and medicine to the affected area. Health workers also educated people about the importance of good hygiene. If you wash your hands regularly, you reduce the risk of infection. If the disease spreads further, more people will get sick. Therefore, it is important to act quickly and effectively.

questions.texte: Analyze the text and answer the questions.
questions.reponse: 1. 'If you wash your hands regularly, you reduce the risk of infection.'; 2. 'If the disease spreads further, more people will get sick.'; 3. They express real possibilities that depend on specific actions or conditions.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify one Type 1 conditional sentence in the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you wash your hands regularly, you reduce the risk of infection.

sousQuestions.texte: Find another Type 1 conditional in the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: If the disease spreads further, more people will get sick.

sousQuestions.texte: What real possibility do these sentences express?
sousQuestions.reponse: They express that certain actions (like washing hands) will lead to specific results, and that the spread of the disease will have consequences.

intitule: Writing - A Short Essay on Disease Prevention

enonce.texte: Write a short essay (5-7 sentences) describing a health problem (e.g., a disease outbreak) and suggesting prevention measures and treatments. Use at least three Type 1 conditional sentences.

questions.texte: Write an essay on disease prevention.
questions.reponse: Example: Malaria is a serious disease that is transmitted by mosquitoes. It causes fever, headaches, and weakness. If you sleep under a mosquito net, you will protect yourself from mosquito bites. If you have symptoms, you must go to the hospital immediately. If the disease is treated early, the patient will recover quickly. Prevention is better than cure. If communities work together to eliminate stagnant water, they will reduce the number of mosquitoes.

sousQuestions.texte: What health problem did you describe?
What prevention measure do you suggest?
What treatment do you recommend?
sousQuestions.reponse: Going to the hospital for early treatment.

sousQuestions.texte: Write one Type 1 conditional sentence from your essay.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you sleep under a mosquito net, you will protect yourself from mosquito bites.
